subject: MATHEMATICS
title: Philippine Money Market
objective: Read and compare schematic peso and centavo amounts.
introduction: One peso equals 100 centavos. Use original tokens, not reproduced banknotes. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Philippine Money Market”.
A key word used in the lesson “Philippine Money Market”.
A key word used in the lesson “Philippine Money Market”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: One peso equals 100 centavos. Use original tokens, not reproduced banknotes.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: One peso equals 100 centavos. Use original tokens, not reproduced banknotes.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

activities.instruction: How many centavos equal one peso?

feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. 10 and 1,000 do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.prompt: Which statement best matches “Philippine Money Market”?

options.text: One peso equals 100 centavos. Use original tokens, not reproduced banknotes.

items.explanation: The correct response is “One peso equals 100 centavos. Use original tokens, not reproduced banknotes.” because it follows the lesson explanation: One peso equals 100 centavos. Use original tokens, not reproduced banknotes.
The correct response is “₱5.00 is five pesos” because it follows the lesson explanation: One peso equals 100 centavos. Use original tokens, not reproduced banknotes.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?
items.explanation: The correct response is “50 centavos = ₱50” because it follows the lesson explanation: One peso equals 100 centavos. Use original tokens, not reproduced banknotes.

items.prompt: How many centavos equal one peso?
items.explanation: The correct response is “100” because it follows the lesson explanation: One peso equals 100 centavos. Use original tokens, not reproduced banknotes.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?
items.explanation: The correct response is “₱12.50 > ₱12.05” because it follows the lesson explanation: One peso equals 100 centavos. Use original tokens, not reproduced banknotes.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Philippine Money Market; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Philippine Money Market.
